narrative: OMX30 opens into a conflicted macro setup: Continental Europe rallies (DAX +0.76%, CAC +0.61%) but Anglo-Saxon markets tumble (S&P 500 -1.22%, NASDAQ -1.34%), resulting in -0.168% correlation signal. Technically, the index sits in a bull regime (+8.2% vs 200-MA, MACD bullish crossover) but stochastic overbought (86.4/75.9) and weak ADX (6.8) suggest fragile momentum. USD/SEK strength (+0.66%) and EUR/SEK appreciation (+0.25%) offer tailwinds for industrials/exporters, yet oil (-0.94%) and gold (-0.97%) weakness remove commodity support. The real risk: overbought conditions combined with thin trend conviction (ADX<18) make this a low-confidence entry; any negative US data could trigger quick reversal.
morningBrief: OMX30 likely opens flat to slightly positive, anchored by European outperformance and USD strength, but expect choppy trading given thin ADX conviction (6.8). Stochastic sits dangerously overbought (86.4 K)—watch for mean reversion dips toward 3105–3115. Exporters (Atlas Copco, Volvo, Ericsson) bid by USD/SEK +0.66%, but NASDAQ weakness (-1.34%) keeps tech sentiment fragile; Hexagon/Ericsson at risk if sentiment deteriorates. Industrials and Banks are modest positives on USD tailwind and NIM relief, but Materials/Mining lack commodity support (Oil -0.94%, Gold -0.97%, Copper -0.25%). Key watch: US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET—any shock could flush overbought longs quickly. Trade cautiously; expect range-bound action 3105–3155 until US data.
drivers: Continental drift: DAX/CAC positive offset by S&P 500/NASDAQ sharp declines

USD/SEK strength supports exporters but NASDAQ weakness weighs on tech sentiment
VIX compression (-8.51%) masks underlying US equity volatility

econCalendarAlert: US Jobless Claims 14:30 CET (watch for reversal trigger on overbought technicals). No major Swedish macro releases. UK political noise (Burnham by-election) low impact for OMX30.
topRisk: Stochastic overbought (K=86.4) + weak ADX (6.8) = high mean reversion risk if US data disappoints or risk sentiment deteriorates further. NASDAQ -1.34% could cascade into tech names. Oil weakness removes floor under industrials if sustained.

RSI 55.86 neutral (not extended; no reversal signal yet)
ADX 6.8 critically weak — reduce position conviction by 30%, avoid aggressive longs
